summary:
Metastatic prostate cancer is uniquely associated with osteoblastic secondary lesions due to its hormonal influence stimulating bone formation. This pattern distinguishes it from other cancers that typically produce osteolytic rather than osteoblastic changes.  

Final Answer: D

Only include content strictly related to the core medical facts about which condition causes osteoblastic secondaries; do not add commentary or extraneous details.

Prostate cancer is identified as the source of osteoblastic metastasis because it stimulates bone remodelling through hormones, leading directly to increased bone formation visible on imaging. Among all listed malignancies, only prostate cancer has been clinically linked to this specific radiological finding of osteoblastic secondaries.
